caseStudy.title: OTA Firmware Update Platform
caseStudy.description: Secure, reliable over-the-air firmware delivery at scale.
caseStudy.overview: The client needed a robust OTA update platform capable of delivering firmware to hundreds of thousands of field-deployed devices with varying connectivity. The platform had to ensure cryptographic integrity, support rollback, and minimize bandwidth consumption.
caseStudy.problem: Firmware updates were distributed manually via USB by field technicians, costing thousands of hours per release cycle. Devices in remote locations went months without critical security patches, increasing vulnerability exposure significantly.
caseStudy.solution: A delta-compression OTA platform was developed with end-to-end signing using hardware security modules. The system supports staged rollouts, automatic rollback on failure, and bandwidth-adaptive chunked transfers. A device management dashboard provides real-time fleet update visibility.

results.summary: Security patches now reach the entire fleet within 48 hours of release. The elimination of manual updates freed up field engineering resources for higher-value work.
